sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Very interesting! After pressing SPACE:
1. The center box (which was G) is now gone!
2. Instead, there's a WHITE node (id=8) at rows 35-39, cols 32-35 with children=[9] (a blue square at rows 37-38, cols 33-34).
3. The white object grew from 4 pixels to 16 pixels (4x4 square).
4. The green N object is still at rows 32-34, cols 32-35.
5. The center box's blue inner square (node 9) is now a child of the white object!
So pressing SPACE while overlapping a G box seems to "collect" the box - the box disappears and the white object grows, containing the blue square that was inside the box.
This changes my understanding of the game:
- The goal is to visit all 3 boxes (making them G) and then press SPACE to "collect" them
- Each collection makes the white object grow
- The level is complete when all 3 boxes are collected
Current state:
- 1 box collected (center)
- 2 boxes remaining (top-right and left, both still c)
- White object now contains 1 blue square
Let me now navigate to another box, make it G, and collect it with SPACE.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# BREAKTHROUGH! SPACE collects the box!
# When green overlaps a G box and SPACE is pressed:
# - The box disappears
# - The white object grows and contains the blue square from inside the box
# 
# Current state: 1 of 3 boxes collected
# Remaining: top-right [24,44]-[27,47] and left [28,16]-[31,19]
#
# Green N: rows 32-34, cols 32-35 (3x4 horizontal)
# White W: rows 35-39, cols 32-35 (5x4, contains blue child)
#
# Plan: Navigate to top-right box, make it G, collect with SPACE
# Then navigate to left box, make it G, collect with SPACE

# Go to top-right box: [24,44]-[27,47]
# From rows 32-34, cols 32-35
# Need: UP ~8 rows, RIGHT ~9 cols
action(['UP', 'UP', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
